title: Audit current JIT architecture and calling conventions
description: Review how existing runtime helpers (jit_rt_string_concat, jit_rt_record_get_field, etc.) are called from ir.rs. Document current parameter passing: which are pointer-based, which need type information, and identify where VM context access is needed. Create a reference document of all 35+ existing extern "C" helpers.
subtasks: List all extern "C" functions in jit.rs
Document parameter and return types for each
Identify which helpers access VM state (scheduler, effects, tools)

title: Design VM context passing mechanism
description: Design how to pass a VM context pointer through the JIT calling convention. Options: (1) thread-local storage, (2) pass as first parameter to all helpers, (3) store in a register, (4) use TLS + parameter hybrid. Evaluate performance implications. This is required for opcodes that need scheduler, effect handler stack, tool provider registry, etc.

title: Migrate existing runtime helpers to accept VM context
description: Update all existing extern "C" runtime helpers (malloc, string ops, record ops, etc.) to accept and use a VM context pointer. This may require creating a wrapper struct or using TLS. Update all call-sites in ir.rs where these helpers are invoked. Create a helper macro to simplify the pattern if needed (e.g., `call_runtime_helper!(helper_name, ctx, args...)`). All 35+ existing helpers must be updated before moving to new opcodes.
subtasks: Update string helpers (concat, eq, cmp, clone, drop)
Update all call sites in ir.rs
Test all existing functionality works with new context

title: Implement ToolCall runtime helper
description: Create jit_rt_tool_call(vm_ctx, tool_id, args_ptr, args_count) → result. This helper interfaces with the ProviderRegistry. Uses VM context to look up tool provider and call it. Returns result or error. Add lowering logic in ir.rs to emit this helper call. Change ToolCall from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Implement tool lookup in ProviderRegistry via VM context
Handle tool call errors and result packing
Add lowering logic for ToolCall opcode in ir.rs
Test with a simple mock tool provider

title: Implement Schema validation runtime helper
description: Create jit_rt_schema_validate(vm_ctx, value_ptr, schema_id) → bool. This helper uses VM context to look up schema metadata and validate the value against it. Returns true if valid, false if invalid. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change Schema from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Access schema registry via VM context
Implement validation logic (delegate to core validator)
Add lowering logic for Schema opcode in ir.rs
Test with sample schema and values

title: Implement Emit event runtime helper
description: Create jit_rt_emit(vm_ctx, value_ptr) → null. This helper uses VM context to access the trace/event system and emit an event. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change Emit from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Access trace system via VM context
Add lowering logic for Emit opcode in ir.rs

title: Implement TraceRef generation helper
description: Create jit_rt_trace_ref(vm_ctx) → trace_ref. This helper uses VM context to generate a new trace reference (span ID, causality link, etc.). Returns the reference as a Value. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change TraceRef from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Access trace system via VM context
Generate trace reference (span ID, parent link)
Add lowering logic for TraceRef opcode in ir.rs

title: Implement Spawn helper for async task creation
description: Create jit_rt_spawn(vm_ctx, closure_ptr) → future. This helper uses VM context to access the scheduler, create a new task for the closure, and return a Future value. The scheduler puts the task in the work queue. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change Spawn from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Add lowering logic for Spawn opcode in ir.rs

title: Implement Await helper for future resolution
description: Create jit_rt_await(vm_ctx, future_ptr) → result. This helper uses VM context to access the scheduler and block/poll until the future is complete. Returns the future's result or error. This is a blocking call. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change Await from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Add lowering logic for Await opcode in ir.rs

title: Implement Perform helper for effect operations
description: Create jit_rt_perform(vm_ctx, effect_id, operation_id, args_ptr, args_count) → result. This helper uses VM context to access the effect handler stack, search for a matching handler, capture the continuation, invoke the handler, and manage the one-shot semantics. Returns the result from the handler's resume() call. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change Perform from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Access effect handler stack via VM context
Add lowering logic for Perform opcode in ir.rs
Test effect dispatch and handler invocation

title: Implement HandlePush/HandlePop/Resume helpers for effect handlers
description: Create three helpers: (1) jit_rt_handle_push(vm_ctx, effect_id, handler_ptr) → null - pushes handler onto effect stack; (2) jit_rt_handle_pop(vm_ctx) → null - pops top handler; (3) jit_rt_resume(vm_ctx, continuation_ptr, value_ptr) → result - resumes captured continuation with value and manages one-shot semantics. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change HandlePush, HandlePop, Resume from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Create jit_rt_resume function with one-shot check
Access effect handler stack via VM context
Add lowering logic for HandlePush, HandlePop, Resume in ir.rs
Update jit_capability() for all three opcodes → RuntimeCall

title: Implement Loop/ForPrep/ForLoop/ForIn as runtime helpers (for now)
description: For Phase 0, implement these as RuntimeCall helpers (deoptimize to interpreter fallback, but mark as supported). Create jit_rt_loop, jit_rt_for_prep, jit_rt_for_loop, jit_rt_for_in that delegate to interpreter. Later phases can optimize these to Native Cranelift IR. This unblocks all cells from JIT compilation even if they use loops. Change Loop, ForPrep, ForLoop, ForIn from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Add lowering logic for all four opcodes in ir.rs
Update jit_capability() for all four opcodes → RuntimeCall
Document that these are temporary stubs for Phase 0

title: Implement In (membership test) and Is (type test) as runtime helpers
description: Create jit_rt_in(value_ptr, collection_ptr) → bool and jit_rt_is(value_ptr, type_id) → bool. These helpers check membership in collections and type tests respectively. Dynamic dispatch based on collection/type at runtime. Add lowering logic in ir.rs. Change In and Is from Unsupported → RuntimeCall in jit_capability().
subtasks: Implement membership test for List, Set, Map
Implement type checking (Int, Float, String, etc.)
Add lowering logic for In opcode in ir.rs
Add lowering logic for Is opcode in ir.rs
Update jit_capability() for both opcodes → RuntimeCall
Test both helpers with various types

title: Remove is_cell_jit_compilable check and enable all cells for JIT
description: Delete or deprecate is_cell_jit_compilable() function. Update all call-sites to always attempt JIT compilation. Since all opcodes now have RuntimeCall implementations (no Unsupported), every cell is compilable. Deopt stubs are no longer needed. Verify test coverage that compilation succeeds for complex cells with loops, effects, tools, etc.
subtasks: Remove or mark deprecated the is_cell_jit_compilable function
Run full test suite to verify
Update any diagnostics that mention 'unsupported opcodes'

title: Delete deopt stub logic and clarify JIT fallback paths
description: Remove code that inserts deopt stubs (jump to interpreter) for Unsupported opcodes. All opcodes are now RuntimeCall or Native, so no deopt is needed. Clarify that if JIT compilation fails for other reasons (e.g., memory), the interpreter is always the fallback (not a deopt stub). Update comments and documentation.
subtasks: Find all deopt stub insertion code
Remove or comment out deopt logic
Update error handling for JIT compilation failures

title: Integration test: compile and run complex cell with all Phase 0 opcodes
description: Write an integration test that compiles and executes a cell using tool calls, effects (perform/handle), async (spawn/await), loops, type tests, membership tests, and emit. Verify that JIT compilation succeeds and execution produces correct results. This validates that Phase 0 is complete and all opcodes are truly working end-to-end.

title: Benchmark: verify JIT performance vs interpreter for all opcode types
description: Create micro-benchmarks comparing JIT-compiled cells against interpreter for: (1) RuntimeCall-heavy cells (tools, effects, etc.), (2) loops, (3) mixed Native+RuntimeCall. Document performance characteristics. Identify if RuntimeCall overhead is acceptable or if future optimization is needed. This informs Phase 1 prioritization.
subtasks: Create benchmark for tool call overhead
Create benchmark for effect handler overhead
Create benchmark for async task overhead
Create benchmark for loop iteration overhead
Create benchmark for membership test overhead
Document results and identify hot paths
Create analysis for Phase 1 optimization priority

title: Documentation: Phase 0 completion summary and Phase 1 roadmap
description: Write comprehensive documentation describing what Phase 0 accomplished: all opcodes now JIT-compilable via RuntimeCall mechanism. Explain the VM context passing design, the pattern for adding new helpers, and performance characteristics. Define Phase 1 goals: optimize RuntimeCall hotspots to Native (likely Loop, In, Is, Await). Include diagrams of calling convention and effect handler stack integration.
subtasks: Document all 16 new helpers added in Phase 0
Create effect handler stack integration diagram
Add code examples for new opcodes in JIT

title: Code review and cleanup: ensure consistency and style
description: Review all new code added in Phase 0 for consistency with existing codebase style. Ensure all runtime helpers follow naming conventions (jit_rt_*), proper error handling, docstring coverage, and safety. Run clippy and fix all warnings. Verify no `unwrap()` calls in library code.
subtasks: Review all extern "C" functions for safety
Add docstrings to all new helpers

title: Update LIR documentation and opcode definitions to reflect JIT support
description: Update opcode_def.rs, SPEC.md, and other documentation to reflect that all opcodes are now JIT-capable (via RuntimeCall). Remove any outdated notes about opcodes being 'interpreter-only'. Update architecture docs to explain the RuntimeCall mechanism and VM context passing.
subtasks: Update SPEC.md section on JIT support
Update ARCHITECTURE.md section on JIT system
Add new section on RuntimeCall mechanism
Add section on VM context passing
Create architecture diagram for JIT system

title: Test suite: unit tests for each new runtime helper
description: Create unit tests in jit.rs for each of the 16+ new runtime helpers. Test normal cases, edge cases (null, empty collections, type mismatches), and error paths. Ensure all helpers are thoroughly tested before integration testing.
subtasks: Create tests for jit_rt_spawn and jit_rt_await
Create tests for jit_rt_perform, jit_rt_handle_push/pop, jit_rt_resume
Create tests for jit_rt_in and jit_rt_is
Test edge cases and error conditions

title: Design VM context struct and access patterns
description: Before implementing any helpers in P0-004 onwards, design the VmContext struct that will be passed to helpers. Define what fields it needs (scheduler, effect handler stack, provider registry, trace system, etc.). Decide on access patterns (methods? direct fields?). Document thread-safety guarantees. This design is critical to success of all subsequent helpers.
subtasks: Decide on access method (methods vs fields)
